top_stories.title: Chinese hackers hijack auth flow, spy on isolated network for a decade
top_stories.summary: Chinese threat actors compromised an organization's authentication infrastructure and maintained persistent access for approximately 10 years. The attackers hijacked the authentication flow to gain full visibility into administrative activity on what was believed to be an isolated network.

top_stories.title: China-linked spies backdoored authentication stack to stay hidden for years
top_stories.summary: A China-linked espionage group called Velvet Ant maintained undetected access inside an unnamed organization's network for nearly a decade by compromising the full authentication stack, according to a Sygnia forensic investigation. The group's control over the authentication infrastructure made detection and remediation exceptionally difficult, highlighting critical risks in authentication system security.

top_stories.title: Securing AI agents in Microsoft Entra with Conditional Access
top_stories.summary: Microsoft is updating its Microsoft Entra identity security framework to address authentication and access control challenges posed by autonomous AI agents. The update involves applying Conditional Access policies to AI agents, as traditional MFA methods designed for human users are insufficient for entities operating at machine speed and scale.

top_stories.title: New attack turned Microsoft 365 Copilot into 1-click data theft tool
top_stories.summary: A vulnerability chain called SearchLeak in Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise could allow attackers to steal sensitive data from a target's mailbox, OneDrive, or SharePoint account via a specially crafted URL. The attack requires no user interaction beyond clicking a link, enabling unauthorized data exfiltration across Microsoft 365 services.

top_stories.title: The rise of machine identities and agentic AI: Securing trust in the next era of digital autonomy
top_stories.summary: A Help Net Security article covers a discussion with cybersecurity expert Chris Hughes on securing machine identities and agentic AI, featuring Delinea's involvement in OWASP's work on non-human identity security. The piece addresses how agentic AI is expanding machine identity challenges within enterprise environments.

news.title: Chinese Hackers Abused Google Workspace Rules to Steal Research and Defense Emails
news.summary: A China-linked espionage group compromised North American medical, academic, and military research networks by installing backdoors on REDCap servers to steal login credentials. The attackers then manipulated Google Workspace email forwarding rules to silently exfiltrate sensitive communications over an extended period.

news.title: SimpleHelp bug lets hackers create rogue remote support accounts
news.summary: A vulnerability in SimpleHelp remote management software allows unauthenticated attackers to create privileged technician accounts on servers using the OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication protocol. The flaw bypasses authentication controls, enabling unauthorized account creation with elevated privileges on affected SimpleHelp servers.

news.title: Delinea and Cyera integrate for data-aware identity security
news.summary: Delinea and Cyera announced a product integration connecting privileged access management with data security posture management. The integration automatically correlates identities—including human, machine, and AI agents—with the sensitive data they can access, enabling security teams to identify and remediate high-risk access paths.

news.title: One-Click Microsoft 365 Copilot Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Steal Emails, Files, and MFA Codes
news.summary: Varonis Threat Labs discovered a chained vulnerability called "SearchLeak" in Microsoft 365 Copilot that allowed one-click exfiltration of emails, calendar data, files, and MFA codes via a legitimate microsoft.com domain link. The attack combined three bugs to bypass traditional anti-phishing and URL filtering tools by leveraging trusted Microsoft infrastructure.

news.title: The Onboarding Password Mistake That Creates Unnecessary Risk
news.summary: The article addresses security risks associated with temporary onboarding passwords, specifically how first-day credentials shared via email or SMS can persist and be reused across accounts. The piece targets IT teams managing new employee account provisioning and access setup processes.

news.title: AI agents are reshaping identity governance, and attackers are already exploiting the gap
news.summary: AI agents are creating new challenges for identity governance as autonomous systems require access credentials and permissions that traditional IAM frameworks were not designed to manage. Attackers are actively exploiting gaps between existing identity governance controls and the access requirements of AI agents in enterprise environments.

news.title: Ex-school district employee jailed for hacks on former employer
news.summary: A former IT employee at an Iowa school district was sentenced to 21 months in prison for conducting cyberattacks against his former employer. The attacks involved deleting accounts and disrupting systems, causing tens of thousands of dollars in damages.

news.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade
news.summary: A China-linked threat group tracked as Velvet Ant backdoored Linux PAM (Pluggable Authentication Modules) and OpenSSH components to maintain persistent covert access for nearly a decade. The compromise targeted the authentication layer itself, allowing the attackers to control who could sign in and survive standard remediation efforts.

news.title: ShinyHunters Exploits Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day (CVE-2026-35273) to Breach Universities
news.summary: ShinyHunters (tracked as UNC6240 by Mandiant) exploited a zero-day vulnerability in Oracle PeopleSoft (CVE-2026-35273) to breach enterprise systems and steal data between May 27 and June 9, before Oracle published its advisory on June 10. The campaign primarily targeted universities, with attackers demanding payment to keep stolen data private.

news.title: Linx Security Integrates with Claude Compliance API to Bring Identity Governance to Enterprise AI
news.summary: Linx Security announced an integration with Claude Compliance API to extend identity governance capabilities to enterprise AI environments. The integration targets hybrid workforce identity management covering human users, AI agents, and non-human identities.

news.title: Microsoft Entra adds device soft-delete and cloud-native sync migration
news.summary: Microsoft Entra has introduced a soft-delete feature for device objects in public preview, enabling administrators to recover accidentally deleted Entra joined or registered devices within a retention period. The update also includes cloud-native sync migration capabilities, with the soft-delete mechanism preserving critical identity and device registration data.

news.title: Levi & Korsinsky Announces Investigation of Securities Claims Against SailPoint, Inc. (SAIL)
news.summary: Law firm Levi & Korsinsky has announced a securities fraud investigation into SailPoint, Inc. following a stock drop of approximately 12% after the company disclosed a weaker-than-expected forward outlook on June 9, 2026. The investigation centers on whether SailPoint misled investors about its growth trajectory prior to the disclosure.

news.title: Teleport Named Most Innovative Identity and Access Management Platform in 2026 Cybersecurity Stars Awards
news.summary: Teleport has been named the Most Innovative Identity and Access Management Platform in The Hacker News 2026 Cybersecurity Stars Awards. The recognition is specifically for Teleport's infrastructure identity architecture, which focuses on resiliency, scalability, and agility for modern computing environments.

news.title: IDMExpress Announces 24×7 Managed Services for Leading IAM/PAM Solutions with Innovative Unified Pricing Model
news.summary: IDMExpress launched a 24×7 Managed Identity Security Operations service covering IAM, PAM, and PKI platforms with a unified pricing model. The service offers a single pool of hours usable across any supported platform with no long-term lock-in contracts.

news.title: Enterprise Identities Are Under Siege. Josys Is Changing How Organizations Fight Back.
news.summary: Josys announced new AI-native platform capabilities aimed at addressing enterprise identity challenges including credential theft, AI agent governance, and SaaS access management. The release targets identity crisis vectors described as credential theft at record scale, ungoverned AI agents, and legacy access policies inadequate for SaaS environments.

news.title: Not all AIs are equal so CIOs need to prioritize actions when assessing risks
news.summary: The article discusses how CIOs should approach risk assessment as AI agents proliferate across enterprises. The piece calls for smarter identity governance and risk-based security controls to manage the varying risk profiles of different AI systems.

news.title: Threat actors are recruiting the people who hold cloud logins
news.summary: Threat actors are actively recruiting insiders who hold cloud platform credentials, turning employees with legitimate access into security risks. The attack vector exploits the credential-based access model of cloud platforms, directly targeting identity and access management controls.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords and seize high-profile accounts, including those of the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official. Instructions for the attack circulated on Telegram, demonstrating a social engineering technique that bypasses traditional account authentication controls via AI-powered support tooling.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, causing a credential leak that CISA is still working to contain. Congressional lawmakers from both chambers are demanding answers as the agency works to invalidate the exposed credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed credentials to highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems via a public GitHub repository. The leaked files included details on how CISA builds, tests, and deploys software internally, representing a significant credential and secrets management failure.
